title: rules.rs duplicates extension/language registry across three constants
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
dry
## Location
src/rules.rs:11-56 (extension_to_language), 147-208 (parse_rule_name, known_languages)
## Violation
The mapping between source extensions and language names is hard-coded in TWO independent constants that must stay in sync (plus a third partial copy in `auto_learn.rs::has_source_extension`/`has_file_extension`). Adding a new language requires three coordinated edits or the rules engine silently degrades.

```rust
fn extension_to_language(ext: &str) -> Option<&'static str> {
    match ext.to_lowercase().as_str() {
        "rs" => Some("rust"),
        ...
}
...
let known_languages = ["rust", "python", "javascript", ...];
```

The `to_lowercase()` allocates a String on every lookup instead of `eq_ignore_ascii_case` (no alloc).
## Mandated refactor
Define ONE `const LANGUAGE_REGISTRY: &[(&str, &[&str])]` mapping language name to extensions. Derive both lookups from this single source. Have auto_learn consume the same registry. Replace `to_lowercase` with byte-wise case-insensitive comparison.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
